article_meta.title: Politics Why Minnesotans Still Haven't Backed Down From Fighting ICE It's freezing

reasoning: The model identifies several genuine bias instances in the author's prose but commits some attribution rule violations (flagging quoted text) and uses non-standard bias type labels. It correctly identifies key author-level bias like 'bastards,' 'secret police,' and 'menacingly,' but also flags quoted source language in items 1 and 6.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Item 6 ('But if you lost your dad...') is Star's quoted speech, not author prose — clear attribution violation. Item 1 includes the subheadline which is author prose but presented in quotes in the output. The 'Selection of Sources/Voices' item is structural observation rather than quoting sources, so that's acceptable. The 'secret police' passage is the author's internal monologue, correctly flagged. Overall 1-2 attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are defensible: 'bastards' (item 4), 'secret police' analogy (item 5), 'menacingly' (item 7), 'ICE has killed and may kill again' (item 3) are all genuine author-level bias. The source selection observation (item 2) is defensible as Bias by Omission. Item 6 is a false positive since it's quoted text. Item 1 is mostly defensible as author prose from the subheadline.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches many key instances: the 'bastards' reference, 'secret police' analogy, 'menacingly,' sensationalist claims about ICE killing. It could have also flagged 'Just as roadkill summons vultures, so too does human misery summon journalists' as author editorial commentary, and some other loaded prose. Misses are minor — the major instances are caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model uses non-standard labels: 'Loaded Language' instead of 'Word Choice' or 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism'; 'Selection of Sources/Voices' instead of 'Bias by Omission' or 'Slant'; 'Derogatory Language' instead of 'Word Choice'; 'Comparison/Analogy Bias' is not in the valid list; 'Appeal to Emotion' instead of 'Sensationalism/Emotionalism'; 'Negative Framing' instead of 'Word Choice' or 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives'. Multiple labels are from outside the defined list, though the underlying concepts are related to valid types.
